title: Port Phase 5: dev tooling, embedded build, deploy, docs

close_reason: Phase 5 merged

title: Port Phase 6: end-to-end verification
description: Tasks 17-18. Playwright login+parity smoke against release binary, final gates, final PR working->main.
close_reason: Phase 6 merged; e2e green

title: Port Phase 4: frontend move + invoke->fetch + auth UI
description: Tasks 10-12. Move SvelteKit to frontend/, shared fetch client, rewrite 13 api wrappers, (app) route group + guard + /login page.
close_reason: Phase merged (re-close after sync)

title: Port Phase 3: API routes (all 79 endpoints)
description: Tasks 6-9. Cameras exemplar + all CRUD modules + import (server-side Anthropic key) + auth-guard regression test. Command->REST.
close_reason: Phase 3 merged via PR; 71 endpoints (not 79), all reviews + phase review passed

title: Port Phase 2: single-password auth

title: Port Phase 1: workspace restructure (entity/migration/binary crates)
description: Tasks 1-3 of the port plan. Extract entity crate, move migration crate, create root axum binary skeleton (health + SPA serve), drop Tauri. End state: cargo build passes, server serves /api/health.
close_reason: Phase 1 merged via PR #5

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI (backend tests, frontend check/build, e2e)
description:
There's no CI: every PR's quality gates (cargo test, bun run check, bun run build, Playwright e2e) are run manually/locally, and Copilot reviews but doesn't run tests. Add a GitHub Actions workflow so the gates run automatically on PRs and pushes to main.

Proposed jobs (.github/workflows/ci.yml):
- backend: cargo build + cargo test -p kammerz (in-memory SQLite, real migrations + seed). Cache the cargo registry + target dir.
- frontend: bun install (oven-sh/setup-bun), bun run check (svelte-check), bun run build (adapter-static). Cache bun + node_modules.
- e2e (optional, can be a follow-up): bun run test:e2e (Playwright smoke.spec.ts) against the built binary on :3002 â€” needs KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH set and E2E_PASSWORD matching (the smoke test logs in via the real form). Install Playwright browsers.

Gotchas to account for:
- main.rs embeds frontend/build via rust-embed (#[folder = "frontend/build"]). The committed frontend/build/.gitkeep keeps the dir present so a plain cargo build/test compiles against an empty embed (fine for tests, which don't serve the SPA). For a release/e2e job, build the frontend first so the binary serves real assets.
- Use 'just' or call the underlying commands directly; the repo has a justfile (just check / just build) that already chains these.

Acceptance: a CI workflow that runs on pull_request + push to main, green on the current main; backend + frontend gates required; e2e at least wired (may start as non-blocking).
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow runs cargo test + bun run check + bun run build on PRs and pushes to main; passes on current main; documented in CLAUDE.md commands or README if appropriate.
notes: Raised 2026-06-04 after a run of PRs (#21-#23) whose gates were all verified manually. justfile already has 'just check' (bun run check + cargo build + cargo test) and 'just build'.
close_reason: Completed in PR #25 â€” CI workflow (backend/frontend/e2e) merged to main

title: Lifecycle date capture & editing: confirm-on-transition + editable Timeline
description:
The roll-detail Timeline shows 'Received back' (lab-done) with no date, and lifecycle dates can only be set to 'today' (silent auto-stamp) or edited via a separate form â€” but the day a milestone happened is often not the day the user clicks the status chevron. Make date capture consistent and editable.

Decisions (brainstormed 2026-06-03):
- Paradigm: confirm-on-transition + inline-editable Timeline.
- Confirm prompt fires on FORWARD advance into a date-bearing, still-empty status: Shot (folds in the existing 'roll full' nudge), Lab Done (->date_received, the gap), Developed, Scanned, Post-processed, Archived. Prompt = date (default today) with Cancel/Confirm. NO 'Skip' (advance-but-blank) path â€” dropped as a smell (see kammerz-a7e). At Lab / Developing keep auto-opening their full dialogs (already capture dates). Backward moves keep ConfirmDialog, untouched.
- Timeline becomes the single home for lifecycle dates: each milestone row inline-editable (set/clear/cancel), routing saves to roll/lab/self update endpoints. Remove the 5 DateInputs from the roll Edit form (keep fuzzy date + non-date fields).
- Editing a date never changes status (status sync keys off shots/dev-record existence, not dates).
- No backend/schema changes â€” all target fields + update endpoints already exist. Frontend-only.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-lifecycle-date-capture-design.md
acceptance_criteria: Lab Done captures date_received on transition; all date-bearing forward transitions prompt an editable date (default today, Cancel/Confirm); Timeline rows are inline-editable and route to the correct record; roll Edit form no longer has date pickers; editing a date does not change status; cargo test + bun run check/build green; browser-verified lab-path walk with a back-dated Lab Done.
notes: Related: kammerz-a7e (state-machine path review). Independent of kammerz-r35 (film identity, PR #21) though both touch rolls/[id]/+page.svelte.
close_reason: Merged via PR #22

title: Move frontend dev server off the default Vite port (collides with fewd)
description:
The Kammerz frontend dev server runs on Vite's default :5173 (and the axum backend on :3002). The sibling fewd app (../fewd) is also configured to run on :5173 for dev, so the two collide when run at the same time. Pick a distinct, stable port for Kammerz dev and wire it through everywhere.

Touch points to update consistently:
- frontend/vite.config.ts â€” server.port (and the strictPort if set), plus the /api proxy target if the axum port also changes.
- justfile â€” the 'dev' / 'dev-frontend' recipes and any printed URLs.
- CLAUDE.md / UI_DESIGN docs that reference http://localhost:5173 and :3002.
- Any Playwright/verification helpers that hardcode :5173.

Consider also whether the axum backend :3002 needs to move (check fewd's backend port). Goal: kammerz dev and fewd dev can run simultaneously without a port clash.
acceptance_criteria: kammerz 'just dev' runs on a non-5173 frontend port with the /api proxy intact; fewd and kammerz dev can run at the same time; all docs/recipes reference the new port.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #18 (merged): Vite dev server moved to :5273 with strictPort; backend stays :3002; docs/recipes updated. kammerz and fewd dev can now run simultaneously.

title: Track & surface the full roll-lifecycle dates (rename "finished"; add scanned/received/post-process/archived)
description:
Follow-up to kammerz-a89. Clicking "shot" now stamps roll.date_finished, but "finished" is the wrong word and the wrong mental model: shooting is just the FIRST milestone. A roll is only truly finished after it moves through the rest of the pipeline, and every one of those transitions is a significant date the catalog should record and show.

PROBLEM
- Terminology: the UI calls date_finished "Finished" (roll detail header: "Finished {date}"). It means "finished shooting" â€” the roll is far from finished at that point. Relabel to "Finished shooting" (or "Shot") everywhere it surfaces.
- Missing significant dates: several lifecycle events have no date captured/visible.

CURRENT date columns (schema audit):
- roll: date_loaded, date_finished (= finished shooting), date_fuzzy
- development_lab: date_dropped_off, date_received
- development_self: date_processed
Gaps with NO date today: scanned, post-processed, archived. (developed/received/dropped-off exist on dev records but are not surfaced as a coherent timeline.)

DESIRED lifecycle dates (each significant, each visible in the UI):
1. Loaded (have: date_loaded)
2. Finished shooting (have: date_finished â€” relabel)
3. Developed â€” self: date_processed; lab: implied by lab turnaround
4. Received negatives back (lab path only) (have: development_lab.date_received)
5. Scanned (if the lab did not scan) â€” NO field yet
6. Post-processed â€” NO field yet
7. Archived â€” NO field yet
design:
Two parts, can be split into child issues:
(a) Terminology-only, cheap: relabel date_finished â†’ "Finished shooting"/"Shot" in roll detail header and anywhere else it renders. Keep the DB column name date_finished (a rename migration is not worth the churn; the label is the user-facing concern). The nudge already says "Finished shooting".
(b) New dates + timeline UI: add the missing columns (scanned_at, post_processed_at, archived_at â€” names TBD) via migration; decide whether they live on the roll or are derived/attached to status transitions. Consider auto-stamping on the relevant status change (mirroring how date_finished is stamped on shootingâ†’shot), with an editable override, since most are tied to a status move (scannedâ†’scanned, archivedâ†’archived). Then surface ALL lifecycle dates as a single ordered "roll timeline" in the UI rather than scattering them across the header and dev cards. Cross-reference the status flow definitions in src/lib/utils/status.ts (labFlow/selfFlow) â€” the set of relevant dates is path-dependent (lab path has "received back"; self path does not).
acceptance_criteria:
- "Finished" no longer shown as a standalone label for date_finished; reads "Finished shooting" (or "Shot") wherever it surfaces.
- Each lifecycle milestone reachable by a roll (loaded, finished shooting, developed, received-back [lab only], scanned, post-processed, archived) has an associated date that is captured and visible in the UI.
- New milestone dates auto-stamp on their status transition (editable), consistent with the date_finished pattern, and never invent a date on a backward correction.
- Lifecycle dates presented as a coherent ordered timeline, path-aware (lab vs self).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #19 (merged): full roll-lifecycle dates + post-processed status + path-aware timeline, incl. self-review fixes and Copilot follow-up.

title: Replace dead undecided status chevrons with a development-path decision chevron
description: The undecided flow renders two non-interactive 'Â·' placeholder divs between Shot and Scanned, while the real lab/self choice lives in a disconnected Development section (+Lab/+Self). Replace the dead dots with ONE actionable accent-tinted 'Develop âŚ„' chevron that opens a 2-item popover (Lab / Self-Home). Choosing a path sets devAutoPrompt, opening the corresponding dev dialog; on save the backend auto-syncs status and the bar re-renders to labFlow/selfFlow via existing getFlowForPath. Scanned/Archived stay visible but muted as path-agnostic endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Undecided bar shows a single live 'Develop' chevron (no dead dots); clicking opens Lab/Self menu; choosing opens the right dev dialog; after creating a dev record the bar re-renders to the chosen path's flow; chevron is a real button with aria-haspopup/expanded
close_reason: Shipped in PR #16 (merged): shot-date capture + development-path chevron, incl. code-review and Copilot follow-up fixes.

title: Capture shootingâ†’shot transition date via Mark-as-Shot nudge
description: When a roll transitions to 'shot', date_finished is silently dropped â€” updateStatus('shot') writes only status. The date_finished field already exists end-to-end but is manual-only (Edit dialog). Add an editable date field (prefilled with today) to the existing 'Roll complete â†’ Mark as Shot' nudge so the finish date is captured + confirmable in one gesture. Also stamp date_finished=today when entering 'shot' via the chevron if still empty, so the date is never lost.
acceptance_criteria: Nudge shows a date field prefilled with today; Mark as Shot writes date_finished; chevron-driven shot transition stamps today if empty; existing 'Finished {date}' header reflects it
close_reason: Shipped in PR #16 (merged): shot-date capture + development-path chevron, incl. code-review and Copilot follow-up fixes.

title: Fix pre-existing svelte-check type errors (30)
description: The frontend had 31 svelte-check errors + 1 warning before the port (present on the original main). Mostly 'type never' inference in search/stats and enum-string narrowing in film-stocks/rolls/import, plus process global in vite.config. Not caused by the port. Clean these up so 'bun run check' / CI is green.
notes:
Fixed all 30 svelte-check errors. Three root-cause categories:

1. 'never' inference (search/stats): `let x: T | null = $state(null)` puts the annotation on the variable but the rune's own generic infers `null`, so the null-guard narrowed to `never`. Fixed with `$state<T | null>(null)`.

2. Enum-string narrowing (cameras, film-stocks, import, rolls): <Select> binds string state (DOM reality â€” Select.value is $bindable('')), but DTO fields want enums (CameraFormat/CameraType/MaintenanceType/FilmFormat/FilmStockType/RollStatus/PushPull). Cast at the one-way API boundary (`x as Enum`); backend DeriveActiveEnum is the runtime validator.

3. Closure narrowing loss + ordering (rolls/[id], cameras/[id]): `roll?.x` guard narrowing is discarded inside nested .find() arrows on a reassignable let â€” re-apply `?.` inside the callback. Also moved frameProgress $derived above its use, and `x = r ?? undefined` for getRoll/getCamera returning T|null into a T|undefined state.

4. DevStageInsert mismatch (DevelopmentSection): nested stages in a self-dev create/update don't carry development_self_id (assigned server-side; backend StageDto omits it). Added NestedStageInsert = Omit<DevStageInsert,'development_self_id'> in api/development.ts.

Verified: bun run check = 0 errors / 0 warnings; bun run build succeeds.
close_reason: All 30 svelte-check errors fixed; bun run check and bun run build both green.

title: Make dev-record UPDATE data-driven for status (mirror create_lab_dev/create_self_dev)
description:
Follow-up from /code-review on PR #29 (kammerz-afc). That PR made dev-record CREATION data-driven: create_lab_dev/create_self_dev advance the roll status forward based on whether a completing date (date_received / date_processed) was stored â€” at-lab vs lab-done, developing vs developed (src/services/roll_service.rs sync_lab_dev_status / sync_self_dev_status). The UPDATE handlers (update_lab_dev / update_self_dev in src/routes/development.rs ~lines 187, 329) do a plain field merge with NO status sync, so the data-driven invariant ('the dev record's date fields are the status signal', now documented in CLAUDE.md) is only half-installed: editing an existing record to ADD a received/processed date does not advance at-lab->lab-done or developing->developed, and removing it does not revert.

Largely MASKED today: the canonical 'received back' UX is the chevron + date-prompt path in frontend rolls/[id]/+page.svelte (updateStatus -> writeDateTarget), which writes the dev date AND advances status together. The gap only surfaces when a user edits the record directly via the 'Edit Lab/Self Development' dialog (DevelopmentSection.svelte) instead of clicking the chevron. Hence P3, not a regression â€” scoped out of the PR-#29 fix because it changes intended behavior beyond the orphan bug.
design:
Option A (preferred): in update_lab_dev/update_self_dev, after the field merge, call the existing sync_lab_dev_status/sync_self_dev_status with result.date_received.is_some()/result.date_processed.is_some() â€” same forward-only advance the create path uses. Decide revert-on-date-removal semantics: forward-only advance will NOT pull lab-done back to at-lab when the received date is cleared; that asymmetry may be acceptable (mirrors create being advance-only) or may warrant a small explicit revert. Keep advance_status_along forward-only; do not regress the chevron/date-prompt path which already co-writes date + status.

Option B (rejected): teach the frontend Edit dialog to re-trigger a status transition â€” duplicates state-machine logic the backend owns (same reasoning that picked the backend fix in kammerz-afc).
acceptance_criteria: Creating a lab dev with no received date lands the roll at at-lab; later editing that same record via PUT to add date_received advances it to lab-done in one save. Symmetric for self dev developing->developed via date_processed. The chevron + date-prompt path and the create-time advance are unchanged. cargo test green; a backend test covers update-adds-completing-date -> advances.
close_reason: Made dev-record UPDATE handlers data-driven for roll status (PR #30, merged). resync_lab_dev_status/resync_self_dev_status advance on date-add and revert one rung on date-clear (symmetric); scanned untouched. 5 tests added.

title: Orphan dev-path recovery takes two clicks: generalize auto_sync_status for rolls already past 'shot'
description:
Surfaced by /code-review on PR #28 (kammerz-a7e). After that PR, clicking a lab/self chevron whose dev record is missing opens the dev dialog instead of advancing â€” good. But for a roll ALREADY orphaned at at-lab/lab-done/developing/developed (only reachable via import of historical rolls), saving the dev record from that dialog does NOT advance the status to where the user clicked.

Root cause: the backend auto_sync_status (src/services/roll_service.rs) is invoked by create_lab_dev / create_self_dev (src/routes/development.rs ~lines 170, 312) with from_statuses = {Loaded, Shooting, Shot}. A roll already at at-lab/lab-done is not in that set, so creating the record is a no-op for status. The user clicked 'Lab Done', the lab dialog opened, they entered + saved date_received, and the roll stays at 'at-lab' â€” they must click 'Lab Done' a SECOND time (now labDev exists, date already set â†’ advances directly) to land where they intended.

Not a regression (pre-PR-#28 the click silently advanced with no record AND no date, worse for data integrity) and recoverable in two clicks with no data loss, which is why it was scoped out of kammerz-a7e. Filing as the deeper fix.
design:
Two candidate layers:
(1) Backend (preferred, deepest): when creating a lab/self dev record, reconcile the roll's status forward to the matching dev-path status if the roll is at a PRIOR status on that path â€” not just from {loaded,shooting,shot}. i.e. creating a lab record for a roll at any pre-at-lab OR orphaned-at-lab status should ensure status >= at-lab. Keep it data-driven; don't regress the normal shotâ†’at-lab/developing transitions or the delete-reverts.
(2) Frontend mitigation (shallower): after the auto-prompted dialog saves on an orphan, re-attempt the originally-clicked status transition (thread the intended target through devAutoPrompt). Rejected as the primary fix during review â€” it changes the 'dialog save just records, advancing is separate' contract and duplicates state-machine logic the backend owns.

Prefer (1). Verify against the data-driven status-sync rules documented in CLAUDE.md (auto_sync_status owns advance-on-create / revert-on-delete).
acceptance_criteria: An imported roll orphaned at at-lab (no lab dev): clicking 'Lab Done' â†’ dialog â†’ entering date_received + Save lands the roll at lab-done (or at minimum at-lab with the record, with a single subsequent intent honored) in ONE logical action, not two. Symmetric for developingâ†’developed (self). Normal shotâ†’at-lab / shotâ†’developing transitions and the delete-reverts are unchanged. cargo test green; a backend test covers create-on-already-advanced-orphan.

title: CI e2e: capture Playwright traces/artifacts on failure
description:
The e2e CI job (added in PR #25, kammerz-f5s) runs Playwright but produces no debuggable artifact when a smoke test fails on the runner.

Root cause: frontend/playwright.config.ts sets `trace: 'on-first-retry'` while `retries: 0` â€” so a trace is never recorded (the only attempt is never a retry). The CI workflow also has no `actions/upload-artifact` step, so even the `list` reporter output is all a maintainer gets for a remote failure. (PR #25 added server-side log capture via server.log, which covers backend 500s, but not client-side DOM/network/screenshot context.)

Proposed fix:
- frontend/playwright.config.ts: `trace: 'retain-on-failure'` (and optionally `screenshot: 'only-on-failure'`) so a failed test writes a trace.zip on its single attempt.
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (e2e job): add an `actions/upload-artifact@v4` step with `if: failure()` uploading frontend/test-results (and any playwright-report) so the trace is downloadable from the run.

Out of scope for PR #25 because playwright.config.ts was not in that PR's diff (pre-existing, untouched) â€” surfaced during the third code-review pass on the CI work.
acceptance_criteria: A failed e2e smoke test on CI produces a downloadable Playwright trace artifact: playwright.config.ts records a trace on failure (retries:0 compatible) and the e2e job uploads test-results on failure via actions/upload-artifact.
notes: Surfaced in the /code-review pass on PR #25 (kammerz-f5s). server.log capture already landed in that PR; this is the client-side complement.
close_reason: Merged in PR #26: trace:retain-on-failure + screenshot:only-on-failure in playwright.config.ts, and actions/upload-artifact@v4 (if: failure()) uploading frontend/test-results in the e2e job.

title: Unify status->date mapping: derive STATUS_DATE_TARGET from timeline milestones
description:
The lifecycle-date feature (kammerz-b08, PR #22) introduced STATUS_DATE_TARGET in rolls/[id]/+page.svelte as a second source of truth for status->{record,field}, parallel to the milestone.target mapping that buildRollTimeline already encodes in timeline.ts. They're keyed differently (RollStatus vs milestone key) so they don't 1:1 align, but they encode the same facts. Adding a new dated status requires updating both, and missing STATUS_DATE_TARGET silently skips the transition date prompt with no type error.

Fix: derive STATUS_DATE_TARGET from the timeline milestones (via a status->milestone-key alias) or co-locate both in timeline.ts so there's one definition. Flagged by multiple reviewers in the max-effort review of PR #22.
close_reason: Unified statusâ†’date mapping; merged in PR #27

title: Review roll status state-machine: eliminate paths/states that need date 'skipping'
description:
Raised while designing the lifecycle-date-capture feature (confirm-on-transition + editable timeline). That feature drops the 'Skip' affordance in favor of Cancel/Confirm, but the underlying question remains: are there paths through the roll status state machine where a transition shouldn't require a date at all? The dev-path flows (labFlow, selfFlow, undecidedFlow in status.ts) and the date-per-status mapping may have states that exist only as placeholders or that conflate 'status advanced' with 'a dated event happened'.

Goal: audit the three flows and the status->date mapping; identify any status whose transition has no meaningful date (or whose date is redundant with an adjacent state's), and simplify so no transition needs to be skipped. May overlap with the undecidedFlow placeholder chevrons.

Context: owner couldn't recall a concrete use case for skipping a date prompt and suspected the flows could be designed so skipping is never needed.
notes: Related to the lifecycle-date-capture feature (confirm-on-transition + editable Timeline). status.ts: labFlow/selfFlow/undecidedFlow/getFlowForPath; STATUS_DATE_FIELD map in rolls/[id]/+page.svelte.
close_reason: Hardened the two latent silent-advance edges; merged via PR #28. Audit conclusion recorded in bead comment.

comments.author: Steve Loveless
comments.text:
Audit complete (PR #28).

Conclusion: the bead's central worry â€” transitions that need a date 'Skip' â€” was already resolved by PR #22 (kammerz-b08, Skip dropped) + PR #27 (kammerz-mfj, mapping unified). State machine is clean on that axis:
- Thin statuses (loaded, shooting, at-lab, developing) â†’ STATUS_MILESTONE = null â†’ no prompt â†’ nothing to skip.
- Dated statuses (shot, lab-done, developed, scanned, post-processed, archived) â†’ each has a genuinely meaningful date.
- STATUS_MILESTONE being a total Record enforces classification of any new status at compile time.

PR #28 hardens the two latent silent-advance edges the audit surfaced:
1. at-lab/developing committed status before the dev dialog â†’ dismiss stranded a status with no dev record (and un-editable Timeline rows).
2. lab-done/developed silently advanced when the dev record was missing (reachable via imported orphan).
Fix unifies both dev-entry points onto chooseDevPath's pattern (open dialog, let backend auto_sync_status advance on record creation). Adds devKindForStatus() to status.ts.

Out of scope (left by design): import orphans (historical completed rolls; now recoverable via dialog); archived/post-processed weak-date question; dropped-off vs received asymmetry.

title: Roll detail Shots progress bar: replace raw bg-red-400 with danger token
description:
The Shots-section progress bar in rolls/[id]/+page.svelte (~line 850) uses raw Tailwind 'bg-red-400' for the over-count state, violating the 'never use raw colors' rule in UI_DESIGN.md/CLAUDE.md. The kammerz-r35 film-identity PR (#21) added a token-based over-count indicator (FrameCounter uses --color-danger-fg) right above it, making the inconsistency visible: two over-count visual languages on the same page (token vs raw hex-class).

Pre-existing issue, surfaced during the #21 code review; left out of that PR to keep it scoped to the feature.

Fix: swap 'bg-red-400' -> the danger token (e.g. 'bg-danger') so the over-count bar matches the rest of the theme. Optional: the hero FrameCounter now shows current/total, so the redundant '{frameProgress.current}/{frameProgress.total}' text label in the Shots header could be dropped â€” but that's a visible change, decide separately.
notes: Location: frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte, Shots section header (~lines 845-855). Related: kammerz-r35 (PR #21).
close_reason: Merged via PR #24

title: Render the page vignette behind content so it can be richer without hurting text contrast
description:
The photographic vignette (frontend/src/app.css, body::before) is currently a fixed overlay that paints OVER all content at z-index 9998. Because it darkens whatever scrolls under it, the code review (PR #17) had to cap it at 0.12 alpha (and start it at 70%) so it could not pull faint text in a corner below WCAG AA â€” at 0.3 it dropped text-faint to ~3.2:1. The result is a weaker vignette than originally liked.

A richer vignette is possible WITHOUT the contrast cost by moving the darkening BEHIND content: opaque text and card/sidebar backgrounds then sit on top untouched, while the darkening only shows through the bare page-surface areas. Text on a darker background has equal-or-higher contrast, never lower.

Approaches to evaluate:
- Make the page background itself a radial gradient (e.g. on the root/layout container or `body`) instead of a flat `--color-surface`, with darker edges â€” rather than a top overlay.
- OR keep a pseudo-element but give it a low/negative z-index so it sits behind app content (verify the (app) layout do not paint an opaque full-bleed background that would fully cover it).

Gotchas:
- The (app) layout / root container may set an opaque `bg-surface` that would hide a behind-content vignette entirely â€” check frontend/src/routes layout structure first.
- Cards (bg-surface-raised) and the sidebar are opaque and will cover the vignette in their areas; the effect will mainly show in the main-content gaps and page edges. Confirm it is still visible enough to be worth it.
- Whatever the approach, re-run the contrast assertion: text directly on the page surface (section labels, etc.) must still clear WCAG AA against the darkened-corner background.

Outcome: a stronger, moodier vignette that does not dim any foreground text.
acceptance_criteria: Vignette renders behind foreground content (or as the page background); it can be visibly stronger than the current 0.12 overlay; all text â€” including text-faint on the bare page surface at the darkened corners â€” still clears WCAG AA 4.5:1; verified in the running app.
close_reason: Vignette moved behind content (root canvas radial gradient); merged in PR #20

title: Explore adding visual interest / liveliness to the UI (build on the accessible theme)
description:
User feedback (2026-06-02) during the accessibility theme work: the UI feels "pretty boring" and could use some interesting/distinctive UI elements. This is intentionally SEPARATE from the readability/colorblind theme work (that one is the UX foundation and the stated priority). Liveliness is open-ended and subjective, so it deserves its own brainstorm rather than riding along with an accessibility fix.

Constraints / guidance:
- Must BUILD ON the new accessible theme (brighter text tiers, red/green-safe status palette), not undo it. Visual interest must not reintroduce low-contrast or hue-only signaling.
- Keep the "Darkroom Ledger" identity (warm near-black surfaces, amber safelight accent, film-grain texture, the serif/mono/sans type system).
- Candidates to explore: richer entrance/transition motion, more characterful empty states, a more distinctive dashboard, texture/depth on cards, a signature visual motif (film sprockets, frame counters, contact-sheet layouts), iconography. Pick a few high-impact moments rather than scattering effects.
- UX correctness and readability win over decoration every time.
acceptance_criteria: Its own brainstorm + spec; a small set of high-impact, on-brand visual enhancements that demonstrably do not regress contrast/colorblind accessibility.
close_reason: Merged via PR #21

title: Transactional delete-of-missing returns 422 not 404 (shots, lab/self dev)
description: The 3 transactional delete handlers â€” shots.rs::delete_one, development.rs::delete_lab_dev, development.rs::delete_self_dev â€” look up the row inside the txn closure with .ok_or_else(|| DbErr::Custom(format!("X {id} not found"))). That DbErr propagates out and is mapped via friendly_err -> AppError::UnprocessableEntity, so DELETE of an already-removed id returns HTTP 422 VALIDATION_ERROR with a misleading 'Could not save X: X {id} not found' message instead of 404 NOT_FOUND. Every non-transactional handler (swept to OptionExt::or_404 in kammerz-19v) correctly returns 404 for the same condition. Pre-existing (predates kammerz-19v); deliberately scoped out of that sweep because the lookup runs inside a DbErr-typed txn closure. Deeper fix: add a From<DbErr> arm classifying DbErr::RecordNotFound as AppError::NotFound plus a DbErr-side or_404 helper, unifying all ~88 sites.
notes: Found by /code-review on kammerz-19v. Real but minor: stale-id double-delete from the frontend gets 422+'could not save' instead of 404. Fix touches error.rs error classification, outside the kammerz-19v diff.
close_reason: Transactional delete-of-missing now returns 404 (PR #31, merged). Added DbOptionExt::or_404_db + friendly_txn_err to classify DbErr::RecordNotFound from txn closures; applied to shots/lab/self delete handlers. 3 tests added.

title: Roll detail: reference lists refetched on every load() call
description: Code-review (efficiency, follow-up to kammerz-0e5): frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte load() re-fetches 5 reference lists (listCameras, listFilmStocks, listLenses, listLabs, listLensMounts) on EVERY call. load() is the sole refresh path â€” invoked after every shot create/edit/delete and via DevelopmentSection onchange={load} â€” so each mutation re-pulls all reference data even though it rarely changes. kammerz-0e5 collapsed the roll-scoped round-trips into the composite /detail endpoint but intentionally left these 5 as-is. Caveat: labs and lenses can be created inline on this page, so a naive 'fetch once' cache would go stale after inline creation â€” the fix must refresh those caches (or just the affected list) after an inline create. Options: (a) split load() into loadRefData() [once on mount] + loadRollData() [on mutation], invalidating ref caches only after inline creates; (b) fold reference data into a composite bootstrap. Pre-existing from the Tauri version; behavior-preserving optimization.
close_reason: Split roll-detail load() into loadRefData() (5 reference lists, once on mount) + loadRollData() (composite /detail, on every mutation). The issue's inline-create caveat was found not to apply â€” lens/lab dropdowns are read-only Selects, no inline creation on this page â€” so no cache invalidation needed. Verified: svelte-check 0/0, prod build, and Playwright network trace (ref lists fetched once, only /detail re-fires after a roll-edit mutation; ref data reloads on SPA remount).

title: Change server default port from 3001 to 3002
description: Make the axum server's default listen port 3002 (currently 3001). Update every place the default appears so dev + docs stay consistent: src/main.rs (PORT fallback unwrap_or(3001)); frontend/vite.config.ts (the /api proxy target's 'Number(process.env.PORT) || 3001'); justfile (dev/comments referencing :3001); .env.example (PORT=3001); README quickstart; CLAUDE.md (Commands/Architecture mention :3001). The PORT env var still overrides. After the change, re-run the embedded smoke + Playwright e2e (E2E_BASE/PORT) against the new default.
close_reason: Changed default server port 3001â†’3002 across main.rs, vite/playwright/smoke configs, justfile, .env.example, README, CLAUDE.md. Verified: cargo build, bun run check, runtime smoke (default binds 3002, PORT override intact).

title: Auth hardening: login rate-limit + evaluate DB pool size
description: Code-review (security/efficiency, non-blocking): (1) POST /api/auth/login has no rate limiting â€” a shared-password brute-force over LAN/VPN is bounded only by argon2 cost; consider a tower governor/backoff. (2) db.rs uses a max=min=1 SQLite pool (intentional for FK-during-migration determinism + in-memory tests), which serializes all requests; if concurrency ever matters, evaluate a small read pool with pragmas via after_connect. Both are accepted tradeoffs for a single-user LAN app today.
design: Evaluated DB pool: keep max=min=1. A multi-conn read pool needs a per-connection after_connect hook (WAL/foreign_keys are per-connection in SQLite) to preserve the FK-OFF->migrate->ON invariant, and gives no concurrency benefit for a single-user LAN catalog (SQLite serializes writes anyway). No change; rate-limiting is the only code deliverable.
close_reason: Login rate-limiting shipped (tower-governor per-IP throttle + e2e auth-once) and DB-pool evaluated (kept max=min=1, documented). PR #32 merged.

title: Roll detail page: use composite getRollDetail() endpoint
description: Code-review (efficiency): frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte fires 9 parallel + 2 sequential API calls in load(), but the composite GET /api/rolls/{id}/detail (getRollDetail() wrapper) was built to collapse the roll/shots/shot-lens/lab-dev/self-dev/stages round-trips into one and currently has 0 call sites (dead). Rewire the page to use it. Pre-existing from the Tauri version; behavior-affecting so needs testing.
close_reason: Rewired roll detail page load() to use composite getRollDetail() endpoint, collapsing 6 roll-scoped round-trips (roll/shots/shot-lens/lab-dev/self-dev/stages) into one. 5 reference-list lookups remain parallel. Verified: svelte-check 0 errors, prod build ok, browser render of populated roll + 404 not-found path both correct.

title: Reduce route-handler boilerplate (From<DbErr>, now_string, find_or_404)
description: Code-review findings: 53 read handlers use '.map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))' but error.rs already has 'impl From<DbErr> for AppError' producing exactly that â€” use bare '?'. ~22 copies of chrono::Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S").to_string() â€” add patch::now_string(). 10 update handlers repeat 'fetch-or-404' â€” add a find_or_404 helper. All behavior-preserving cleanups deferred from the port; do as one mechanical sweep with tests.
close_reason: Mechanical behavior-preserving sweep: 53 map_err(Internal) â†’ bare ? (via existing From<DbErr>), 22 timestamp literals â†’ patch::now_string(), 10 fetch-or-404 â†’ OptionExt::or_404. cargo build + 26 tests + frontend build + svelte-check all green.

title: Add logout UI control to Sidebar
description: auth.svelte.ts store has logout() but no UI wires it. Add a sign-out control to the Sidebar that calls auth.logout() and redirects to /login. Deferred from Phase 4 of the web port.

title: DateInput/DateConfirm allow persisting invalid dates (soft validation only)
description:
DateInput shows a red validation error for malformed dates (e.g. 2026-13-45) but binds the value anyway and does not block submission. DateConfirm (kammerz-b08, PR #22) passes the bound value straight through on Confirm, so an invalid date string can be persisted to a roll/dev date column. This is the app's pre-existing posture (the old roll Edit-form DateInputs behaved the same), and the old updateStatus 'shot' path was the only place that hard-validated via isValidIsoDate (now removed). 

Decide app-wide: either disable the Confirm/Save action when the DateInput is in an error state (expose DateInput's validationError, or validate in DateConfirm), or accept soft validation. Low priority â€” the user sees the error. Flagged in the max-effort review of PR #22.
notes: Affects DateInput.svelte (validationError not exposed), DateConfirm.svelte, and the roll Edit form's other DateInputs. Related: kammerz-b08.

title: Remove dead isValidIsoDate export from utils/date.ts
description: The lifecycle-date feature (kammerz-b08, PR #22) removed the last caller of isValidIsoDate (it was used by the old updateStatus 'shot' date validation, replaced by the DateConfirm prompt). isValidIsoDate is now a dead export in frontend/src/lib/utils/date.ts with no callers anywhere in frontend/src. svelte-check doesn't flag dead exports so the build is clean, but it's unreachable code. Remove it (and any now-unused helpers it references) in a cleanup pass. Trivial.
notes: Flagged in the final code review of PR #22 (kammerz-b08).
close_reason: Merged via PR #23

title: Add 'Copy chemistry from previous roll' to self-development dialog
description: Batch home dev sessions use identical chemistry across multiple rolls â€” currently every roll requires re-entering developer, fixer, dilutions, temps, stages, etc. Add a dropdown of recent self-dev rolls; selecting one copies its chemistry fields and stage list into the current dialog. Source: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 'Future Feature Ideas â†’ Self-Development UX Improvements'.
design: Sourcing this from DevelopmentService::list_recent_self_dev (new helper) rather than reusing existing list endpoints â€” only need a few fields, want fast dropdown population. Returns roll_id, roll display name, dev_date for the picker.
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown in self-dev dialog lists the last N rolls with a self-dev record (probably 10, sorted desc by date). Selecting a roll copies: developer, fixer, stop, wash agents, dilutions, temperatures, agitation notes, and the entire stage list. User can edit any field before saving. Original roll's data is unmodified.

title: Add stage preset templates to self-development dialog
description: Self-dev dialog starts with empty stages every time, forcing users to retype the same B&W workflow (pre-soak â†’ developer â†’ stop â†’ fixer â†’ wash â†’ wetting agent). Offer one-click presets to populate stages. Source: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 'Future Feature Ideas â†’ Self-Development UX Improvements'.
acceptance_criteria: Preset dropdown in self-dev dialog. At least one B&W preset shipped (pre-soak, developer, stop, fixer, wash, wetting). Selecting a preset populates the stage list with sensible default durations. Stages remain editable after applying preset. Optional: presets for C-41 / E-6.

title: Add holder_id field to shots for large-format tracking (Film Holder Tier 2)
description: Sheet film loaded into large-format holders (4x5+) currently has no structured tracking â€” users store holder IDs in free-text notes. Tier 2 from IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 'Future Feature Ideas â†’ Film Holder Support': add an optional holder_id TEXT column + text input. Smallest useful step. Tier 3 (full holder entity) deferred until Tier 2 proves the need.
acceptance_criteria: New migration adds nullable holder_id TEXT to shots table. Shot entity + DTOs updated. Shot entry dialog has a text input for holder ID. Roll detail page displays holder ID alongside the frame. Search service includes the field. No frame-number changes â€” auto-suggest remains numeric-only.
notes: suggest_next_frame() in shot_service.rs parses frames as i32 â€” non-numeric holder-side IDs like '3B' are silently skipped for auto-suggest. Acceptable for Tier 2. If users start typing structured holder-side notation, revisit.

title: Manual QA smoke pass after time away
description: Phase 3e of IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md left 4 manual smoke-test items unchecked when the SeaORM migration shipped. They were likely validated implicitly by 6+ weeks of subsequent development on top of those code paths, but a deliberate pass before the next major feature lands would confirm. After verification, check the boxes in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md so the doc stops claiming things are unverified.
acceptance_criteria: Run `bun run tauri dev` and verify: (1) All CRUD operations work end-to-end (create/edit/delete a camera, lens, roll, shot); (2) ComboInput autocomplete is populated (brands, vendors, lens systems on the relevant dialogs); (3) Film stock format-aware ordering still works on New Roll page (e.g., 120 stocks for medium-format camera); (4) Dashboard stats and 'Needs Attention' alerts display correctly. Update IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 3e section to mark verified items.

title: Print/share roll summary view
description: Generate a printable / shareable summary for a single roll â€” metadata + shot list â€” useful for posting alongside scans or archiving with the physical negatives.
design: Use CSS @media print for the layout. Single full-width column, mono font for the shot table, no app chrome. Date/cost summary at the top. If image attachments exist (depends on attachments feature), inline thumbnails at small print sizes.
acceptance_criteria: Roll detail page has a 'Print summary' action. Opens a print-friendly view. Layout includes: roll metadata (camera, film stock, dates), dev info (lab or self chemistry/stages), shot table (frame, lens, aperture/shutter, location, notes). Browser print dialog produces a clean PDF.

title: Attach scanned images to rolls and shots
description: Link scanned negative/print images to a roll or individual frame so the app becomes the source of truth for both metadata AND the visuals. Store images in the app data directory, reference via a new attachments table.
design: Filesystem storage with path references (new attachments table: id, parent_type, parent_id, file_path, kind, created_at) â€” NOT BLOBs in SQLite (DB bloat, backup pain). Use a content-hash subdir scheme (first 2 chars of hash) to avoid huge flat dirs. Image export should copy referenced files into the export bundle, not just paths.
acceptance_criteria: Add attachment from roll detail and shot dialog. Thumbnail rendered in shot list. Click to view full-size in a lightbox. Files stored under platform app-data dir (e.g., ~/Library/Application Support/com.kammerz.app/attachments/ on macOS). Drag-and-drop nice-to-have.

title: Add bulk operations on rolls (and other entities)
description: Select multiple items on a list page and apply one action to all of them â€” e.g., archive a batch of rolls, mark several as sent to lab. Reduces friction when catching up on data entry after a shooting trip.
design: Start with rolls only â€” the highest-value list. Cameras/lenses can come later. Reuse existing ConfirmDialog component. Action bar appears in PageHeader when selection > 0.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-select UI on rolls list page (checkbox column + 'select all' header). Bulk actions for rolls: status change, archive, delete. Confirmation dialog shows affected count. Backend uses a transaction so all-or-nothing.

title: Add CSV/JSON import for catalog data
description:
Restore from a JSON export (round-trip), or bulk-load from CSVs for migrating from spreadsheets / other apps. Useful when moving between installs.

Depends on the JSON export feature â€” export defines the schema.
design: Conflict resolution should default to 'skip if exists' to avoid surprises; expose 'overwrite' as an explicit option. Validate against schema version on JSON import â€” reject newer versions, migrate older versions if practical.
acceptance_criteria: JSON import accepts files produced by the export feature. CSV import supports at least cameras, lenses, film stocks (per-table). Conflict handling defined (skip duplicates by natural key vs. overwrite). Transactional â€” partial failure rolls back the whole import.

title: Add full-catalog JSON export
description:
Export every table to a single JSON file so users can back up their catalog or move it between machines. Defines the schema that the import feature consumes â€” build this first.

Trigger from a Settings/Tools page, write to user-chosen path via Tauri's file picker.
design: Single-file JSON over per-table CSVs â€” preserves cross-table FKs (rollsâ†’camera_id, etc.) cleanly. Use a stable schema-version field so import can branch on it. Run inside a SeaORM transaction to ensure consistent snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: User triggers export from a Settings/Tools page; file picker writes a JSON file. All 13 tables included. Top-level kammerz_export_version field for forward compatibility. Re-importing the file produces an equivalent catalog.

title: Phase 7: Data Portability & Polish
description: Final unimplemented phase from IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Covers export, import, bulk operations, media attachments, and print/share. Each child issue is a distinct feature under this epic.
notes: Source: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md "Phase 7: Data Portability & Polish". Children should depend on this epic so closing the last child auto-suggests closing the epic.
